extensions.description: Uncompressed audio files used for character dialogue and environmental sound effects.
Compressed audio format typically used for PlayStation 2 specific sound data.
3D geometry and animation data for characters, NPCs, and physics objects.
AI pathfinding nodes that tell NPCs where they can move within a level.
Compiled map and geometry data defining the physical structure of game levels.
User interface graphics, such as menus, buttons, and sliders.
2D animated sprites used for HUD icons, glows, or muzzle flashes.
A package file that bundles multiple game assets into a single storage file.
Game-specific data files for level logic and localized text strings.
Small graphic files used for menu icons and save-game indicators.
Standard bitmap image files used for static graphics or textures.
Lump files, usually containing raw bitmap data for menu backgrounds.
System metadata files, often describing contents of save folders.
Likely font data or system settings for platform-specific rendering.
Plain text files containing credits, level titles, and chapter lists.
Script files that set console variables, game difficulty, and controls.
Resource script files used to initialize engine parameters at startup.
Precache data used to load assets into memory before they are displayed.
Used for surface decals like blood, scorch marks, and bullet holes.
